reviews.title: Absolutely Deplorable Experience – Avoid at All Costs!
reviews.text:
My recent stay at the Ritz Carlton Singapore was an absolute nightmare. The service was beyond abysmal, with rude and incompetent staff who seem to have no clue about how to treat guests. Any attempt to provide feedback was met with hostility and defensiveness, as if they were incapable of acknowledging their own shortcomings.

For a hotel at this price point, the service should be impeccable, but it was anything but. Save yourself the trouble and go to the Four Seasons, which is leagues better in every aspect. The laundry service was a complete disaster—they handed back clothes in a worse state than before and had the audacity to act like this was acceptable after being asked to redo them.

We paid extra for the club lounge, only to be given the wrong access cards for over a day, yet they still charged us for the full experience. To top it off, hot water didn't work, the room was filthy, and they couldn't even get my friend's name right, despite it being the second booking. It was embarrassing.

The minibar? We asked five times for it to be replenished, and it never happened. Trying to get a hold of anyone to fix these issues was next to impossible. The incompetence was staggering, and it became clear that they simply do not care about their guests.

We ended up leaving early due to the sheer horror of the experience and are now planning to sue for a full refund. The general manager should be ashamed of the state of this hotel—it's an utter disgrace to the Ritz Carlton name. Avoid this place like the plague.

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.negatives:
Having recently stayed at the Ritz-Carlton Singapore, I must confess my disappointment with the overall experience, especially considering the exorbitant price tag attached to the stay. While the hotel boasts a prestigious reputation and is positioned as a five-star establishment, I found the disparity between cost and quality quite glaring.

Firstly, the outdated facilities and decor were evident throughout the property. Despite its esteemed status, the hotel appears in dire need of renovation, with worn-out furnishings and aging infrastructure detracting from the luxury ambiance one would expect from a Ritz-Carlton establishment.

Moreover, the room rates charged seemed unjustifiable when compared to the amenities and services provided. While I understand the allure of a prime location in Singapore, it hardly justifies the premium pricing, especially when rival hotels offer comparable accommodations at a fraction of the cost.

Additionally, the level of service fell short of the standards one would anticipate from a five-star hotel. While staff were polite, their efficiency and attention to detail left much to be desired. Simple requests often took an inordinate amount of time to be fulfilled, and the overall guest experience felt lacking in personalization and warmth.

In conclusion, while the Ritz-Carlton Singapore undoubtedly holds a prestigious reputation, it fails to deliver commensurate value for the exorbitant prices charged. Prospective guests would be wise to explore alternative accommodation options that offer superior quality and service at a more reasonable cost.

reviews.title: Big rooms but too pricey for its offered amenities
reviews.text: For a US$493 per night's room rate x 4-nights, the amenities included are not enough, considering that Ritz Carlton is a 6-star branded hotel.  WiFi inside the room is charged separately, if you want complimentary, you need to go to their lobby and use it there.  No nail clippers available when I needed one.  They still want you to buy from their shop if you need one during your travel.  Their F.O. is not as friendly as you would expect in a 6-star hotel.  One reason the F.O. gave was that I booked my room thru an online agent and that my booked rate was not a good deal for them.  I wonder why do they use this as a reason for not providing good service?

1. Other than the very good breakfast and the way over-priced Chinese restaurant, there are no reasonable dining options particularly one is vegetarian. In our case we had to go out for reasonably priced vegan options for most meals. 
2. While the staff is quite good, in our opinion the hotel is too big and crowded to provide a nice environment and experience at this price point. Elevator crowding and wait times and the super crowded breakfast area are examples. 
3. In going to the bar for a cocktail, it was very annoying to be handed a menu with all drinks in the $125 to $250 range! It took the wait person a while to produce a menu with more reasonably priced options. Really surprised and irritated to be upsold in this way. 

For these reasons we are unlikely to choose RC in the future.

The TV didn't work and kept resetting itself for two days. I suppose they fixed it on the 3rd day when we were out because it worked on the 3rd day. reviews.text:

Requested for scissors from concierge, which took approx 20 to 30mins to be delivered to the room. In room dining also not available after 1030pm. Location was ok, but apparently easiest pick up point for grab is at marina Square 711 instead of hotel lobby?

Last gripe - granted this may be my own doing (for not reading the instructions..) but as we were checking in on national day, there were many road closures. Called to enquire how to get to the carpark, but was not instructed clearly. Ended up having to make 2 detours before another service staff that attended to our call, mentioned that entrance to the carpark was through millenia walk. 

Was simply expecting more. Everything was kinda dated and not up to par with other hotels we've visited for the same if not less, price point.  will revisit.

Secondly, I had requested for a birthday cake to be sent up on a particular timng on the day i arrived. a day before I checked in, and they said it could be done. Upon check in, I reminded them again for the same request, which they said they had noted. And upon reaching the hotel room, I reminded them again regarding the request and they said ok. But at the end of the day, there was no birthday cake and nothing was being arranged. When I called to asked they told me "no arrangement was made" 
How is this possible when I have reminded them 3 times and all 3 times I was given an "ok" regarding my requests. What is this??? 

I don't understand how this kind of standard and service is coming from a "6-star" hotel, I would highly suggest that they should start improving on their standards (and update the precise room photos on the website to prevent guests from being misled by the wrong information)

reviews.title: Worse 5 star hotel ever!
reviews.text:
I must say, I've never come across a more appalling example of a so-called '5-star' hotel. My experience was nothing short of a nightmare, but what tipped the scales into the realm of the unforgivable was their laughable attempt at service recovery. Their solution? To pacify you with a complimentary stay, hoping you'll swallow your dissatisfaction and stop making a fuss. They'll go through the motions of CC'ing their upper management, but don't be fooled - none of them will lift a finger.

I took the initiative to email their high-and-mighty senior management, but my efforts were met with a deafening silence by Riri Yufriane Apriliani (Assistant Director of Rooms) and James Png (Front of House Manager). What followed was a month-long email exchange, a convoluted dance of empty promises and unfulfilled expectations. Douglas Heng, a name I'll remember for all the wrong reasons, had the audacity to arrange a callback that never materialized. And would you believe it, they had the gall to apologize and promise another callback - a callback that, unsurprisingly, never came.

I possess a damning catalogue of their email responses, a paper trail of their incompetence. Rest assured, I will not hesitate to expose this farce to the public.

reviews.title: Once Great service but now only good
reviews.text:
We choose Ritz- Carlton over Pan Pacific because of it great service reputation. However we must said in comparison to Pan Pacific Singapore ( which is just around the corner from the Ritz- Carlton) the service leave a lot to desire. I arrived at the hotel around about 12:30PM for check in. We was quickly escorted to the check in counter by a very polite female staff at the door.
The receptionists seem under pressure,with only two staffs at the counter. One staff was looking after two guests on one side and the others was Japanese receptionist busy dealing with some Japanese guests. The lady escorted us was trying hard to "radio" someone to come assist our check in. It took a good 3-4 minutes. Finally another lady come from some way. While she was assisting us, phone was ringing non-stop but all receptionists was too busy to attend to it. After a good 15 minutes the receptionist then told us , sorry our room is not ready ( which is Understandable, given most chek in at hotel is 14:00), but it is most be appreciated to be told earlier rather then 15 minutes later. 
When we are checking out, again the hotel reception was really busy with a long line of people waiting. We then approached a girl that walk past us, she then "radio" someone and organise us to check out from another reception. It is clear that the reception is poorly designed for dealing with large number of people at a given time, or simple it is poorly staffs by the management. We recently stayed at Park Hyatt Tokyo, they have a staff escorted us straight to the room and have all our registration in the room , so was the case  at Ritz- Carlton Jumeira Dubai, perhaps something the Ritz-Carton Singapore management team can learn from. 
The other let down is the servicce in the restaurant.It's not attentive. We have to prompt for service all the time.  All other service was very good and the telephone opeartor was in fact great. It is a shame the check in and out and restaurant service pull down the rest of

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.positives:
We came all the way to Singapore to celebrate National Day, but the night was way more crowded than we expected especially around Marina Bay Sands. We ended up stuck in the crowd, and my partner had a panic attack. He wasn’t feeling well afterwards, and by Sunday, August 10, he was completely down.

At first, we were given late check-out until 2 PM, but after I explained our situation, the reception kindly extended it to 3 PM. That extra hour really helped, and after resting more, my partner felt so much better. I’m really grateful for that.

The food was delicious, we loved our in-room breakfast. Dinner was nice too, although the nachos weren’t quite to our taste. The view from our room, even without the Marina view, was still gorgeous. Overall, I truly enjoyed my first stay here.

Thank you so much for the warm hospitality

reviews.title: Disappointed, poor staff service
reviews.text:
This hotel is overprice, unless you are a caucasian, you will not enjoy quality service here at Ritz Carlton Singapore. As asian, will have to wait in line even though u checked in first before caucasian. 

Also, do note that I was there at 2:30pm even though the check in time was 2pm as state, though most hotel allows check in at 12pm if rooms are available. I had to wait a good 45mins before getting a room card. I also had to keep checking back with the front desk staff instead of them approaching me seated within sight.

I quote another occasion where I was waiting inline at the concierge, infront of me was a caucasian who is able being served. When another staff comes along, he offer his service to the guest who is already being served before attending to me when directed by the staff serving the guest.

I was made to feel like a second class guest. Not to mention that the television was abit faulty. It would shut off by itself after 15mins of viewing. Also, there is no ipod connector nor boss sound system for a luxury hotel. Room service took it's time too. Other that the view, I did not enjoy this hotel. 

If you would prefer a hotel which value your business and professionally trained staff, I would recommend St Regis Singapore if you don't mind not having the view.

reviews.title: Average experience
reviews.text:
My friend and i got the xmas package which includes breakfast for 2 and guaranteed late check out at 3pm (as stated on the booking page).. upon checking in, I was told that my xmas package does not entitle to late check out.. the front desk officer assisting me was very kind to call her superior to check.. and I took out my voucher print out to check, true enough, there isnt any statement that say guaranteed late check out.. 

Nevermind the late check out, coz what really matters to us is the view of the room as we wanted a weekend getaway.. the view is magnificent especially at night!  

An hour after checking in, my friend called the reception to check if we are entitled to the ritz carlton club.. what she was told from the receptionist is that we are entitled to enjoy the facilities at the ritz carlton club.. but when We reach the club, the lady told us politely that our room is not entitled to the privilege and We need to pay SGD 150 per pax for it! 

Nevermind the miscomm.. We went back to our room, And My friend wanted to charge her hp.. she opened one of the drawer looking for a plug.. and she found chocolate wrappers left by the previous guest!

This is totally intolerable from a five star hotel! We called the reception to complain.. the receptionist told us that she will get the housekeeping to clear the rubbish in another 10 mins.. We actually planned to head out for dinner, but they Kept us waiting for 20mins, but no One came! We Called housekeeping this time, and it took them 10mins to send someone to clear the chocolate wrappers!

This is rather frustrating and totally not justifiable not the price we paid! Otherwise, the bedding were very comfortable and the breakfast is good and very filling.. overall I would say its only an average hotel experience..

reviews.title: Average, could have been better
reviews.text: I booked a one night staycation at the Ritz Carlton, knowing that I was going to expect a lovely stay. However there were many small issues that annoyed me, and it was a fairly unpleasant stay for me. Firstly, I was given a room on the 13th floor - I didn't think much of it cos hey it's just one night, I can put my superstitions away (plus, they gave me a corner room so that's great!) Next, the shower/bath tub taps were being weird. The slightest toggle of the tap gave me either icy cold water or REALLY hot water. I had to spend 5 minutes adjusting it (and burning myself in the process) before I got the right temperature. Ignored it cos the toilet was too pretty to care about anything else. So i called the maintenance guy, and he came up within 15 minutes (that was a rather long wait). He came into the room and walked about without removing his dirty boots, and there were boot prints all over the floor. So this is just 1 hour into my stay at the Ritz - I was really annoyed. The next morning, I requested for an extra set of toiletries (ran out of soap) so I could take a shower and check out on time. The housekeeping staff took 1 whole hour to get the soap to me, and of course, this wasted an extra hour of my time. Other than the not-so-pleasant experiences, the room is really beautiful. I'm just not sure if i'd come back to the Ritz again.

reviews.title: normally a fantastic hotel, this time however not!
reviews.text: normally a fantastic hotel, having stayed a few times there, this time however a disappointment related to the Ritz Carlton Club. Having paid 150USD surplus for the Ritz Carlton Club however and being told at arrival that the Ritz Carlton Club which is typically located at 32nd floor with gorgeous views on Marina Bay etc being closed for the rest of the month 'due to a VVIP' and relocated to 1st floor is not acceptable. The 1st floor location had no view, was totally worn down, air-conditions leaked, not an appropriate replacement at all. Whoever this VVIP was, having still doubts on that explanation moreover, Ritz Carlton should think twice of accepting hundreds of typically loyal but non VVIP guests being as much as disappointed as we were as trade off for satisfying a VVIP! thinking twice now on returning to the Ritz especially after the previous Ritz-Carlton experience in Kuala Lumpur was already a disaster.

reviews.title: Fantastic service in a great location
reviews.positives: The service at the Ritz Carlton is second to none. The staff greet you with a smile (and often, remarkably, remember your name!) and nothing is ever too much trouble. Our room was sensational - large, comfortable and with an amazing view over Marina Bay. We didn't have breakfast included but went to the buffet breakfast at The Colony twice. It offers an incredible range of options, however, best to avoid at weekends and peak times due to the crowds. We weren't familiar with the location of the Ritz Carlton prior to arrival but would thoroughly recommend it as it was so easy to navigate the city and sights from here.
reviews.negatives: If you're a coffee lover, would recommend looking at options outside the hotel, as the options available here are expensive and not the best quality. There a lots of good options in the nearby shopping areas.

3rd times staying in Ritz Carlton. The standard and service drop with each stay.
I remember during my 1st stay which was a 5 years back, the service and amenities were super and exceeded 5 stars. This stay the service and amenities are normal 4 stars hotel standard. The furnishings are old and worn out, the wooden flooring even turn black at certain area (suppose to he brownish wooden color). When i washed the kettle trying boil some water, but I saw black residue keep coming out. When I show to the housekeeping, his reply is these are from the exterior of the kettle not the internal so it is ok.
Overall I feel that based on this experience I will not select Ritz Carlton for the next stay as the price we are paying we are expecting a much more higher standard of service

reviews.text:
Everything looked great upon arrival. Check in was fast (well, it was midnight), the room was great, very spacious, the view was superb. But sad to say, half the good point was destroy the next morning when we had breakfast. It was served at the ball room, with limited choice of selection. They don't even have thousand island for salad dressing! During 3 morning I had my breakfast there, I had to asked for a glass of drinking water and waited for at least 10 minutes and after asking for several waiter. Come on!! it's only 2 glass of drinking water. Not everyone have juice or tea or coffee in the morning. 
So Ritz Carlton, sorry. you are not for me.

- The BF was really big spread but definitely doesn't worth S$45 per person before tax. Food was just slightly above average. 
- The pool was shocking disappointing small and very crowded that i don't even bother to step in.

- The room was huge, the bed and 6 pillows were superb that my husband sais that he want to buy Seally for our new home.
- The bathtub with views were really a 'wow' and relaxing but becos we had a room that bathtub is facing the office towers, we can't leave the shades up to enjoy the view were in the tubs. *sad

reviews.title: Close to Perfect Stay
reviews.text:
Ritz Carlton lives up to its reputation of excellent service. All aspects in terms of comfort, cleanliness, safety, location (many nearby food options, malls, transport and attractions), facilities (clean, not too cold or crowded pool and/or gym) are well done. Even kids are taken care of with adequate activities and amenities to enjoy.  

If there's anything to nitpick, it would be the property is dated, where wooden floor boards have become creaky, toilet bowls do not come with bidet or warming, toilet has only 1 basin (though it has abundant table top space and wall hooks). Beds are placed just beside the door, which is disrupting to all occupants, if 1 of guests moves in/out the room.

reviews.title: Great city break hotel
reviews.text: Had a great stay at the Ritz Carlton. We were slightly dissappointed on arrival to be told that there was an event going on in the area & there could be noise between midnight & 4am - not exactly what we wanted after a 9hr flight. Staff were apologetic & provided ear plugs but luckily we slept through and no noise was heard.  Hotel itself was in a great location & room had amazing views of the marina from the bedroom & bathroom. Bed was very comfortable & staff were very helpful and attentive throughout our stay.  The breakfast caters for all tastes & whilst at times we needed to queue for a table the staff worked very hard during those busy periods.  We ate in the restaurant and pool area both meals were of a high standard. All up a great stay & would recommend it if you're looking for somewhere away from the hustle & bustle of Orchard Road.

reviews.title: Nice place for staycation
reviews.text:
I decided to do staycation in Singapore and I wanted to try Ritz as I was curious about the hotel.
After checked in we were happy with the room size and my daughter was so excited and she kept walking around the room to explore.
The TV variation is really poor though, there was only 2 channels for kids and it wasn't very good, luckily I brought a portable DVD player for my daughter in case she wants to watch something.
The wifi is still chargeable, which is unfortunate, I believe nowadays hotels should provide free wifi to their guests.

We had great time in pool, it was spacious and service was always attentive but not intruding.
The hotel is connected to Millennia walk and Marina Square, so in case you don't want to eat in the hotel you can walk to any of these 2 malls for other variety of restaurants.

We also ordered in room service for breakfast the next morning, it was delivered on time and everything was good.

Overall, we had nice time and definitely will come back in the future :)

reviews.title: Ritz   carlton    Singapore x
reviews.text: The  room was Noisy  I   could   hear   people    walking  outside,   At   night    could   hear   taps   being   turned  on  ,   could   hear   the   Fidge   /   airconditioning    at   night .  They    did    move    me   to a   new   room   which  was   better    but   not     noise  free. The   room   was not   luxurious  enough   ,   it     was   not   as   luxurious   as    one   expects   Ritz   Carlton    to  be  ,  I   stayed   at  a   Ritz   carlton  in  the   US  it   was    much   more  Luxurious.The   Check in   was   smooth  ,  the   staff    were  very  professional    and    courteous ,   I   was     escorted   to   the   room   which  was   very   good .  The   loocation  of   the   hotel  is   very    good   and    convenient   ,   views     are   excellent.  The   restaurants    are   very   good,   the   chinese   restaurant   Summer  Pavilion   had   excellent   food. It   would   be   good    to  have  a   Western   restaurant   like   a   good   steakhouse    in   the   Hotel

reviews.title: Central location with a spectacular view
reviews.text:
Was a fantastic stay just for the weekend. The suite was spacious and affords us a spectacular view of the Marina! It happened to be National Day rehearsals so the view was even better including the fireworks at night right in front of our eyes!
The service and the spread at the club lounge was superb with food layout anytime of the day!
Location of the hotel was very good and convenient. Easy access to the city. Also nearby the Marina Square mall and Millenia walk and Sumter city mall offers ample restaurants and shops!

reviews.title: Magnificently memorable
reviews.text:
Our sty at the Ritz was memorable and fitting for the occasion. I brought my girl on my business trip because she turns 7 on my departure day. Thus, decided to book a good hotel to celebrate her 7th. Likewise, my best friend with me on my trip was also celebrating her birthday a week after. Thus It was a double celebration. I informed the hotel and they happily upgraded us to a suite room. Despite some confusion in our reservations (long story) we got our rooms with terrific view of the river and all the attractions there is in front of us. Much more, the weekend was a preparatory event & drills for the up and coming national day thus we had a great show in front of us without going anywhere. My daughter totally enjoyed the spectacular view and show of the drama, jets and sky divers, fireworks & light shows, etc while enjoying dinner from room service. The hotel also gave her a birthday cake and a card on the house. Though the hotel is grand and beautiful, with superb staff, including the door man... !!!.... food was not great. Thats the only downside. I hope they improve on it.  The hotel is connected to the Millennia walk and Marina Square, all are shopping complexes with everything you need. From there it connects to other parts nearby and also the MRT station. It is a huge complex of hotels and malls linked together with an underground mtr that makes it so convenient. From the front, taxi is easily available, the singapore flyer is just a 5 mins walk away. Marina bay sands is just across 10 mins by foot. In general, I would definitely recommend this hotel for its great location, great staff, great service, beautiful and large rooms with a nice river view if you book the rooms on the Marina bay view side. Security is excellent as well. Room keys are necessary to be able to make the lifts work. 
Thanks to the staff, Anita from the front desk, Clara from the customer support, Habsah from the reservations office. My daughter said that it was a very memorable and bes
